data.name: spellslayer drake
data.lore: Spellslayer Drakes may be the most enchanting and graceful of their kin to behold, but they are also the most shrouded in mystery. Possessing a keen intelligence otherwise lacking among their relatives, they dwell in ancient ruins seeped in old magic. They've even been observed to consume all matter of spells directed at them with relish.

data.name: orc witchdoctor
data.lore: Orcs are naturally drawn to the forbidden art of Necromancy. Witchdoctors take this one step further by empowering themselves with the enslaved souls contained within the charms carved from the skulls of their victims. Witchdoctors can reanimate and control the bones of fallen warriors and drain the life-force of their enemies to heal themselves.

data.name: skorn grandmaster
data.lore: Skorn Grandmaster title is reserved for those in the Warlock ranks who hold the twisted honor of being the most corrupted by demonic influence. In battle they are a force to be reckoned with, casting powerful magic infused with demonic energy, weakening their opponents and raining chaos upon all who oppose the Lord and the coming of Pandemonium.

data.name: manastorm djinn
data.lore: Deep within the puzzling temples of Hadarak Desert, the Manastorm Djinn lie in wait. Holding ancient knowledge beyond the understanding of mortals, these powerful creatures have tapped into the secrets of sorcery, wielding an array of destructive spells to obliterate any curious intruders.

data.name: hell gazer
data.lore: Hell Gazers prefer to live in the most devastated areas, where poor settlers barely scrape by in ramshackle shelters and meager rations of food. In these places, the feeling of sorrow and hopelessness lies heavy in the air, providing ample nourishment for these malicious creatures.

description.text: {Mirror Images, #FBFB79} now deal {%.1f%%, white} increased damage.

translation.pt-BR: {Mirror Images, #FBFB79} causa {%.1f%%, white} de dano adicional.

data.name: warlock ezanor
data.lore: Rumored to be the bastard son of Roland Godberd, Ezanor was born sickly and unfit to wield a weapon. This prompted him to delve into tomes of dark and arcane knowledge, becoming adept at reinforcing his vitality by sapping his enemies of theirs. He went down such a dark path to prove himself to his father and the Brotherhood. Was it all worth it?

data.name: saltdusk bloodmage
data.lore: While blood magic is shunned by most people, it is a preferred medium for these raiders to fuel their violent endeavors. Powered by the blood forcefully taken from prisoners in the deepest cells of the keep, Saltdusk Bloodmages wield spells that leech their victims and reinvigorate themselves to keep fighting till the last drop.

data.name: harvester hag
data.lore: Harvester Hags are the corrupted remains of sorceresses that lost their children. They have odd bells hanging from their cloaks for luring children away from safety. When they get close, the hag uses its phylactery to siphon away their souls, feeding upon their innocence instead of craving for flesh like her sisters.

description.text: When targets affected by your {Shackles of Pain, #FBFB79} are {healed, #77D463}, you are also {healed, #77D463} by {%.1f%%, white} of that amount.

data.name: bone gazer
data.lore: The dark eye of a Bone Gazer is enough to paralyze even the fiercest adventurers in fear, and that's not even considering the creature's row of jagged teeth. These Gazers are known to gather minerals from the environment to grow and strengthen their bonelike faceplates.

data.name: crow matriarch
data.lore: Insidious creatures corrupted to their very core, Matriarchs are the oldest and most powerful Hags of the Crowhollow Bog's covens. Delving deep into the dark arts for centuries has made them ruthlessly effective at both commanding other Hags and ensuring the designs of their ancient patron come to fruition.

description.text: Reduces the cooldown of {Hex, #FBFB79} by {%.1f seconds, white}.

translation.pt-BR: Reduz o Tempo de Recarga de {Hex, #FBFB79} em {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: contagious sporewalker
data.lore: Once, they were a man that got lost in the swamp, or taken from their home by the Crowmaidens. Whoever they once were has now been replaced by a fungal network that controls their body like a puppet. Despite appearing fragile physically, the fungus uses this apparent weakness as a means to spread its spores when damaged.

translation.pt-BR: Se {Death Touch, #FBFB79} eliminar o alvo, reduz o Tempo de Recarga da habilidade em {%.1f%%, white}.

data.name: troll warlock
data.lore: Troll Warlocks are a perfect example of how even the dull-minded can tap into the arcane. Their magic prowess comes innately, it's well known that Trolls can't read books, or don't care to. These brutish spellcasters can cast blazing mana bolts or focus protective shields to repel incoming damage, even command the ground below to collapse.

data.name: chainwraith haunt
data.lore: The catalyst of addiction and obsession birthed these restless spirits. It is said that the intensity of the heat that their lanterns emit correlates directly with how strongly their passions controlled them in life. They eternally chase the highs that once made them feel alive.

data.name: goblin wizard
data.lore: Goblin Wizards are among the most intelligent of all Goblinoids. These Goblins use magical means to achieve their goals of havoc and thievery. Their spells include hurling fireballs, self-healing, and perhaps most devious of all - transporting money from your pockets into their own.

data.name: hog saboteur
data.lore: With little regard for life or limb, a Hog Saboteur charges into battle with lit explosives strapped to its body. Throwing dynamite at friend and foe alike, these crazed beasts care more about causing destruction than much of anything else. Even other Warhogs know to keep their distance from the maniacs.

data.name: emberglow trunk
data.lore: Haunted trunks are possessed by the distressed woodland spirits seeking retribution wherever nature has been wronged. The Emberglow Trunk is the fiery embodiment of their scorching rage, often causing wildfires that wipe out greedy lumberjacks and encroaching farmers. A tragedy for both sides, but nature can always rise stronger from the ashes.

data.name: draconic pyromancer
data.lore: Having sworn loyalty to their master, Vemurat, in ages past, these once proud Ellesset Elves traded away their traditions and freedom for the promise of draconic power. Having become imbued with the fires of their volcanic home, they're both expert Pyromancers and utterly resistant to the heat.

data.name: coal contraption
data.lore: While Goblin engineering often falls behind that of the Dwarves in regards to sophistication and ingenuity, the machines built by the green-skinned inventors are second to none when it comes to sheer destructive power and volatility. The Coal Contraption is a noisy, six-legged, fire spewing automaton designed for mayhem.

data.name: goblin bomber
data.lore: The Goblin Bomber undergoes a truly euphoric experience in the moment they witness the explosions they're responsible for. Their mad giggling carries even over the loudest of battlefields and the only thing that amuses them more is when they inflict "accidental" friendly fire.

data.name: emberscale drake
data.lore: The wildly temperamental Emberscale Drakes make their nests in inhospitable places like volcanic fields or the lava-teeming caves within the depths of the earth. Able to breath flames reaching impossible temperatures, they can quickly incinerate and melt everything standing before them, be it rock, steel or flesh.

data.name: flamelord djinn
data.lore: It is said that the older a Djinn gets, the stronger it becomes. If true, then the flamelords have been around for millennia. Burning with deep, almost infernal-like, flames, they have total control over any fire source. When their blistering gaze meets their prey, only one thing is certain: ashes will be their only remains.

data.name: goblin pyro
data.lore: A Goblin Pyro is obsessed with destruction by fire. As a result of this singular fixation, most of these Goblins are missing fingers, hands, or entire limbs. Always holding bombs and other explosives, it's simply not recommended to be within throwing distance of these green-skinned pyromaniacs.

data.name: wildfire jackal
data.lore: The Wildfire Jackal is the result of attempts to imbue the ancient power of Firelord Djinn onto the Runegust Jackals. Although the experiment proved successful it, perhaps, yielded more than intended. This new breed appears more powerful and more aware of said power, unwilling to merely be tools for lesser beings.

data.name: astor demon
data.lore: Born out of the madness infused realms of Pandemonium, Astor Demons and all others of their ilk possess an unquenchable thirst for inflicting terror and destruction upon all life they encounter. This desire is so strong that even the most violent of orcs and trolls despise the demons sensing their nature.

data.name: zorian hailbringer
data.lore: Like Stormcallers, Zorian Hailbringers have a tight control over the water element. But they have learned to focus on the particularly chilling aspect of the substance. They are capable of launching sharp frostbolts or conjuring up deadly freezing hailstorms. When walking over land, a thin layer of ice forms wherever a Hailbringer steps.

data.name: icicle gazer
data.lore: Icicle Gazers have an innate understanding of the manipulation of pure ice and, therefore, can shape it to their will, even manipulating the environment around them is a feat any wizard can only dream of!

data.name: coldstone cub
data.lore: Unlike their kin elsewhere, Coldstone Foxes do not have the luxury of play or room for error. Even while Cubs, they demonstrate an unwavering instinct to fight and to survive. While they may have a cute appearance, their bite can turn fingers to ice.

data.name: froll cryomancer
data.lore: Only the most magically talented Frolls can become Cryomancers, shamans with the ability to bend cold and ice to their will, which they use to craft devastating weapons for their brethren. They mate for life and when one dies, the other will carry their severed head so that the pair is never separated.

data.name: frostrisen magus
data.lore: Enduring years of corruption and decay from the White Blight has erased any trace of humanity from the minds of those who once were wise and skilled spellcasters. What's left is something unnaturally dangerous, made even more powerful by exposure to Igneous Ice.

data.name: snow broodmother
data.lore: A Snow Broodmother's poison is considered to be one of the coldest known substances, instantly freezing almost anything it comes into contact with. They set up webs made from a thick crystalline material that hardens as it cools, refracting light and making their lairs look like they are lined in beautiful, icy gemstones.

The skill's cooldown is reduced by {%.1f seconds, white}.

translation.pt-BR:
{Frost Lance, #FBFB79} agora possui {3 cargas, white} e deixa de consumir Aether e ter seus efeitos adicionais. {Frost Lance, #FBFB79} causa {%.1f%% (Spell Power) de dano m�gico, #BA7DFF}.

Reduz o Tempo de Recarga em {%.1f segundos, white}.

data.name: shadowfox ninetails
data.lore: Shadowfoxes are mysterious, nocturnal creatures that generally shy away from contact. Often mistaken for wraiths because of their skull-like faces and glowing eyes, Shadowfoxes are more active at night, using their excellent vision to hunt prey. All Shadowfoxes gain power from the moon, growing stronger as the sun goes down.

description.text: When teleporting, a {Frost Nova, #FBFB79} occurs where you teleport from, {slowing, #FFA851} targets by {%.1f%% for 3 seconds, white}.

translation.pt-BR: Ocorre {Frost Nova, #FBFB79} no local do qual voc� se teletransportou, que causa {%.1f%% de Lentid�o, #FFA851} aos alvos por {3 segundos, white}.

data.name: lurking shadow
data.lore: Inhabiting the Fields of Despair, these lithe and agile creatures live in the shadows, watching unsuspecting travelers from a distance. When their quarry appears most vulnerable, they'll utilize their teleportation abilities to manifest out of the darkness and strike!

description.text: After teleporting, you gain Surge of Magic, increasing your {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} by {%.1f%%, white} for {6 seconds, white}.

translation.pt-BR: Ap�s se teletransportar, gera Surto de Magia que aumenta seu {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} em {%.1f%%, white} por {6 segundos, white}.

data.name: iceshell giant
data.lore: Only the strong thrive in the cold habitats of Glaceforde. Not all Hatchlings will survive to become Giants, but those that do are powerful enough to hold their own against Snow Spiders and Blizzard Beasts. Iceshell Giants have the ability to shoot a wickedly sharp icicle from their backs.

description.text: Applies Arctic Burn to the target for {6 seconds, white}, dealing {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} every {2 seconds, white}.

translation.pt-BR: Aplica Congelamento Intenso por {6 segundos, white} nos alvos atingidos, que causa {%.1f%% (Spell Power) de dano m�gico, #BA7DFF} a cada {2 segundos, white}.

data.name: frostbound drake
data.lore: Frostbound Drakes are, perhaps, the largest of the Drake family, and the most aggressive. They live in harsh, extremely cold climates and do not tolerate competition, even from others of their kind. Their breath is capable of freezing the target into a solid ice statue.

description.text:
You gain a stack of Arctic Piercing for each target hit by {Coldblast, #FBFB79}, lasting 8 seconds.

Arctic Piercing increases the {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} of your {Frost Shards, #FBFB79}, {Frostbolt, #FBFB79}, {Frost Lance, #FBFB79} and {Ice Storm, #FBFB79} by {%.1f%%, white} up to {5 stacks, white}.

translation.pt-BR:
Gera um ac�mulo de Perfura��o �rtica para cada alvo atingido por {Coldblast, #FBFB79}, que dura {8 segundos, white}.

Perfura��o �rtica aumenta o {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} da pr�xima {Frost Shards, #FBFB79}, {Frostbolt, #FBFB79}, {Frost Lance, #FBFB79} e {Ice Storm, #FBFB79} em {%.1f%%, white} (M�x. {5 ac�mulos, white}.

data.name: iceforge frostcaller
data.lore: The prolonged exposure to Igneous Ice changed not only the Frostcallers' bodies, making their eyes glow like stars of frigid blue, but also empowering them with the ability to control ice and snow. Folk swear that they're not only capable of wielding the elements, but that the beasts of Glaceforde answer their songs.

description.text: {Icestorm, #FBFB79} no longer requires to be channeled but deals {%.1f%%, white} of the damage.

translation.pt-BR: {Icestorm, #FBFB79} deixa de ser uma habilidade canalizada, mas causa {%.1f%%, white} do dano.

data.name: blizzard beast
data.lore: The Blizzard Beast is a ruthless carnivore stalking the frozen wastes of Glaceforde. They have perfectly adapted to their harsh environment, possessing a thick fur coat and a keen predatory intellect. Local Glacefordians sometimes even refer to them as Devil-Hounds for their red eyes and large horns.

description.text: Ice shards fall twice as fast. {Icestorm, #FBFB79} deals {%.1f%%, white} more damage.

translation.pt-BR: Estilha�os de gelo caem duas vezes mais r�pidos. {Icestorm, #FBFB79} causa {%.1f%%, white} de dano adicional.

data.name: tiger bug soldier
data.lore: Tigerbug colonies are believed to sprawl miles below the surface, yet no expedition has returned successful in mapping one. Most of these daring efforts meet their end crushed beneath the pincers of a Tiger Bug Soldier, the hulking guardian of the hive, tasked with dealing with any intruder intending to reach its deeper chambers.

description.text:
Nature's Strike inflicts the target with Swarming Insects, dealing {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} every {3 seconds, white} for {9 seconds, white}.

This effect does not stack and successive applications only refresh the duration.

data.name: mammoth
data.lore: Standing high and mighty as the uncontested ruler of the snowy plains, the Mammoth is a majestic beast to observe, but only at safe distances. Fiercely territorial and protective of their young, a pack of these wooly behemoths can cause destruction comparable to an avalanche.

description.text: Every third cast of {Nature's Strike, #FBFB79} also {slows, #FFA851} your target by {%d%%, white} for {3 seconds, white}.

data.name: poisonpetal thornmaw
data.lore: Some scholars believe Poisonpetals have endless variants, as these creatures can rapidly adapt to their environment. The Poisonpetal Thornmaw being one such case, many theorize it forms when multiple individuals fuse together into a far deadlier entity. Each head seems to act on its own, adding weight to that theory.

description.text: Instead of applying damage over time, when Bubonic Spores reach {3 stacks, white} they explode after a second, dealing {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} to the target and its surroundings. This effect can only occur once every {15 seconds, white}.

data.name: elderwood ghostoak
data.lore: Originally named in Elvish as "Nature's Wrath", legend says an Elderwood Ghostoak is born when its grove falls to the hands of men. Fueled by endless rage and magical energy sourced from the essence of decay, these grim avengers will stop at nothing until balance is restored.

description.text:
Targets afflicted with Bubonic Spores no longer receive additional damage from Spore Spread. Instead, they are {slowed, #FFA851} by {%.1f%%, white} per charge.

Upon reaching 3 charges, the target is also {snared, #FFA851} for {%.2f seconds, white}.

data.name: orca
data.lore: Ask any seasoned sailor about the sea's most dangerous denizens, and they'll name the Orca as the true apex predator. "Sharks are big and strong, but not much brains on them..." they say. "Orcas, though? Clever, ruthless creatures, and they hunt for sport!"

description.text: {Life Touch, #FBFB79} creates a Magical Barrier equal to {%.1f%% adaptive power, white} on the healed target for {6, white} seconds. While the Barrier is active, the target is {immune, white} to {Fear, #FFA851}, {Blind, #FFA851}, {Confusion, #FFA851} and {Stun, #FFA851}.

data.name: thousand-eyes trunk
data.lore: Experienced adventurers know to keep an eye out when traveling the wilds, and the Thousand-Eyes Trunk preys on those who overlook that teaching. What seems like a tranquil resting spot near a lone dead tree can turn to chaos in an instant, when its countless eyes snap open, the madness begins.

description.text:
The damage of {Life Touch, #FBFB79} is increased by {%.1f%%, white}.

Additionally, reduces their {(Weapon Power), #FF5151} and {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} by {%.1f%%, white} for {4 seconds, white}.

data.name: black wolf
data.lore: Black Wolves dominate the food chain wherever they roam. Larger and stronger than lesser wolves, even a lone one is a formidable foe. Unfortunately they rarely hunt alone, as these cunning beasts form highly organized packs, each led by a single, ruthless alpha.

description.text: {Bristlehide, #FBFB79} also increases the target's {Movement Speed, #C2DBFB} by {%.1f%%, white} while any shield is active.

data.name: cragglehide
data.lore: Gentle yet territorial, Cragglehides are often found in pairs, forming strong lifelong bonds with their mates. These affectionate creatures work together to defend their shared territory from potential threats, using their imposing size and strength to deter intruders.

description.text: When Bristlehide's Barrier is destroyed or expires, it shoots its bristles out, and the target deals {%.1f%%, white} of the Barrier's total value as spell damage to enemies around itself.

data.name: high elf sorcerer
data.lore: While all elves share a bond with nature, High Elves extend theirs to the cosmos itself. High Elf Sorcerers often study the ebb and flow of stars and celestial bodies, drawing arcane power from the very fabric of the universe. The oldest and wisest among them often become Elders that guide the elven people.

description.text: {Solar Flare, #FBFB79} no longer confuses affected targets. Instead, Solar Flare's base cooldown is reduced by {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: morningstar missionary
data.lore: In the distant wilds where Mornginstar expeditions push forward, the Missionary proves invaluable. Endless jungles and swamps can wear down a soldier's spirits, but these fervent preachers sustain both faith and discipline, converting heathens as zealously as they remind their brothers and sisters of their righteous path.

description.text: {Solar Flare, #FBFB79} heals you and allies within its range for {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463}.

data.name: petrified trunk
data.lore: Folktales in forest regions often talk of the spirits dwelling in ancient woodlands, and many stories tell of how vengeful they can be when crossed, even holding grudges for centuries. The force animating this Petrified Trunk must be extremely powerful, wrathful, or both.

description.text: Targets {slowed, #FFA851} by {Thorn Wall, #FBFB79} also become {snared, #FFA851} for the first {%.1f seconds, white} of the effect.

data.name: sand spider
data.lore: Crafty and resourceful, Sand Arachnids adapted to the desert's harsh expanses, where they thrive as opportunistic hunters. Anything that stumbles into their traps, be it great or small, is fair game. Their bite delivers a horrific venom said to drain every drop of moisture from their prey, leaving only a brittle husk behind.

description.text: Enemy targets damaged by {Thorn Wall, #FBFB79} will receive a condition that takes {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} every {2 seconds, white} and have their healing received reduced by the same amount for {10 seconds, white}.

data.name: orc chanta'
data.lore: An Orc Chanta's rite of passage begins at a young age, when he carves his totem from massive chunks of wood and bone, forging a deep mystic bond with it. The ritual ends only when he becomes strong enough to wield it with ease, both as a weapon and a conduit of primal magic.

description.text: Party members affected by {One with the Hive, #FBFB79} also receive a Shield equal to {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463}, lasting for {6 seconds, white}.

data.name: piranha
data.lore: Individually, a Piranha is a small, unremarkable fish. Together, however, they become a ravenous tide of teeth and hunger. Swarming with frenzied precision, they can strip flesh from bone in seconds, leaving nothing but a red stain on the water. By the time you notice them, it's already too late.

description.text: Enemy targets poisoned by {One with the Hive, #FBFB79} also have their {Movement Speed, #C2DBFB} reduced by {%.1f%%, white} for {12 seconds, white}.

data.name: elf bladedancer
data.lore: Moving with the grace of a falling leaf and the precision of a striking viper, the Elf Bladedancer turns battle into an art form. Weaving through enemies with effortless finesse, her blades strike faster than the eye can follow. By the time her foe realizes the fight has begun, it is already over.

description.text: {Queen's Charge, #FBFB79} also increases basic attack damage of both you and your party members by {%.1f%% Adaptive Power, yellow}.

data.name: mossback colossus
data.lore: Towering over its kin, the Mossback Colossus stands as a pinnacle of strength, commanding respect among the Stonelings as the earth quakes beneath its steps. Bearing the weight of ages, these titans embody nature's endurance, standing sentinel against any who dare disturb their ancient domain.

description.text: {Queen's Charge, #FBFB79} also generates a Barrier on you equal to {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463} for each party member affected by the skill.

data.name: skeleton warrior
data.lore: The fine craftsmanship of their armor is beyond the skill of any human blacksmith, a relic of a forgotten past. Their fighting style feels antiquated, as if these Skeleton Warriors are still locked in a battle long lost to time. How many centuries have they wandered, waging an ancient war that ended ages ago?

description.text: Enemy targets {snared, #FFA851} by {Living Roots Swarm, #FBFB79} also have their {(Defense Power), #FCBE7F} reduced by {%.1f%%, white} for {2.5 seconds, white}.

data.name: elf paladin
data.lore: Elves often turn to direct combat only as a last resort, so Elven Paladins hone their diplomacy and oratory skills as much as their martial abilities. But when words fail, those who stand against the elite of elven forces should remember: These warriors train for centuries, and elves have very long lives.

description.text: The Barrier on friendly targets from {Living Roots Swarm, #FBFB79} is also increased by {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463}. Additionally, you receive a Barrier of {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463} for {6 seconds, white}.

data.name: molebear
data.lore: Molebears are timid by nature, preferring to avoid humans, until someone wanders into their tunnels. Completely blind from a lifetime in darkness, they rely on a sharp sensitivity to the slightest vibrations in the earth, detecting both prey and trespassers with incredible precision.

description.text: Targets hit by a Blooming Sapling when it blooms are also {snared, #FFA851} for {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: serafine
data.lore: Oh Serafine, princess in red, lady of night's seduction, Perfumed in decay, draped in dusk's corruption. Call the revel once more! To drink, to dance, to drown in dark! Where crimson fountains freely flow, one last waltz before they part.

description.text:
Thirsty Sapling applied by {Blooming Sapling, #FBFB79} no longer drains {Maximum Mana, #52A8F7} of its target. Instead, becomes a Bloody Sapling, making the enemy target suffer {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} every {2 seconds, white} for {10 seconds, white}.

After {10 seconds, white} have passed, Bloody Sapling blooms, dealing {300.0%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} to the target and to enemies within a small area around it.

data.name: earthwarden
data.lore: Stealthy and cunning, the Mossolith excels in ambushing prey using its mossy, stone-like exterior for camouflage. Dwelling in rocky terrain, it feeds on small creatures and vegetation. Mossoliths often mark their hunting grounds with distinctive scratches on tree trunks and rocks.

description.text: When casting {Avatar of Rebirth, #FBFB79}, each healing over time effect active on yourself, up to a maximum of 3 effects, increases your {(Healing Power), #77D463} by {%1.f%%, white} for the duration of the effect.

data.name: ancient warden
data.lore: The oldest elven legends speak of powerful spirits that once shaped the world and acted as its protectors, only to vanish without a trace. Deep within the Elder Coast lies a grove where one such being sleeps, the Ancient Warden. Beyond the veil, it walks dreams within dreams, plagued by nightmares in an endless struggle to keep them at bay.

description.text: Allies healed by {Avatar of Rebirth, #FBFB79} also receive a Barrier of {%.1f%% (Healing Power), #77D463} for {4 seconds, white}.

data.name: dusk crawler
data.lore: Although similar in appearance and hailing from Pandemonium just like their Astor kin, Dusk Crawlers hold themselves to a higher standard. Their destructive tendencies are not expressed for the sole purpose of destruction, but to assert their dominance over others. To subjugate and force any who oppose them into submission.

description.text: {Avatar of Decay, #FBFB79} marks enemy targets for {6 seconds, white} each time it deals damage to them, and upon reaching {3, white} marks, they are consumed and the target is {silenced, #FFA851} for {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: thergardian hero
data.lore: Time is ruthless, erasing flesh and turning bone to dust, but for those cursed with undeath, it takes much more. The Thergardian Hero still walks the halls of his lost kingdom, driven by a will that's not his own. Perhaps beneath his cold, lifeless gaze lies a remnant of his old self, an ancient champion longing for an opponent capable of granting rest.

description.text: While under the effect of {Avatar of Decay, #FBFB79} you leave behind a trail of decay that lasts for 4 seconds. Enemies walking over the trail are {slowed, #FFA851} by {40%%, white} for {2 seconds, white} and receive {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF}.

data.name: blood spawn
data.lore: In clear mockery of traditional golems and elementals, the Blood Spawn is born when a master of blood magic animates a pool of tainted blood to do their bidding. This twisted creation is tied to sacrificial rites and vampiric cults, a perverse defiance of life itself.

description.text: Every third shot now becomes a Blood Shot, which no longer reduces the target's {(Weapon Defense), #FCBE7F} but instead heals you for {%.1f%% (Weapon Power), #FF5151}.

data.name: blaze spider
data.lore: These arachnids can often be found in the most extreme and unwelcoming environments, near areas of high volcanic activity or in the scorching wastes of the Hadarak Desert. A Blaze Spider's venom burns like molten lava, melting skin and muscle as it flows through the bloodstream.

description.text: Every third shot now fires an incendiary round, burning the target for {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} every second for {3 seconds, white}.

data.name: spikehorn drake
data.lore: Closely related to the Direhorn Drakes, the Spikehorn is a bulkier, stronger and far more aggressive beast. Forming packs of only a few individuals, they prefer to nest in hard to reach places, like mountains and cliffs, from where they can dominate vast territories.

description.text: The second shot from {Double Tap, #FBFB79} also {slows, #FFA851} the target by {%.1f%%, white} for {2 seconds, white}.

data.name: high elf sniper
data.lore: Fabled among the elven forces, High Elf Snipers are said to fire arrows that never miss their target. One tale tells of a merchant who offended a sniper, only for the elf to shoot an arrow into the sky and walk away. Years later, the merchant walked the street, when a lone arrow fell from above and pierced his skull.

description.text: Both shots from {Double Tap, #FBFB79} now ricochet to enemies within a large area around the primary target, each dealing {%.1f%%, white} of the damage dealt to the original target.

data.name: reiner blitz
data.lore: Bound only by his own code, Reiner Blitz values strength above all else. He disdains petty rulers, laws and kings alike. He firmly believes that those who seek power must possess the strength to claim it, wield it, and enforce it. Those who lack such strength, should face the harsh consequences of their weakness.

description.text: After being pushed back by {Scatter Shot, #FBFB79}, drop a bomb at your original position that will explode after {1.5 seconds, white}, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} to targets in a small area around itself.

data.name: skeleton veteran
data.lore: Even the greatest of heroes eventually retire to live out their golden years in peaceful respite, often training the next generation of fighters. For some, however, that's not the case. The Skeleton Veteran proves that some warriors refuse to let even death end their career.

description.text: {Scatter Shot, #FBFB79} also leaves a line of tar on the ground in front of you for {3 seconds, white}. Enemies that walk over it are {slowed, #FFA851} by {%.1f%%, white}.

data.name: morningstar smiter
data.lore: When far from Firslight, in distant shores and strange lands, a Smiter serves as jury, judge, and executioner. Tasked with keeping the peace and enforcing the Hierophant's orders, they wield both faith and justice, delivering fiery judgement to those who dare defy the Morningstar law.

description.text: {Grappling Hook, #FBFB79} now drops a bomb at your original position that will explode after {1 second, white}, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} in a small area around itself and {slowing, #FFA851} affected targets by {30%%, white} for {2 seconds, white}.

data.name: asterion
data.lore: Asterion claims to have been the first of his kin to emerge from the bowels of the Underearth and to have never seen the light of day. The uncontested ruler of his labyrinthine prison has a taste for the blood of the brave and bold. Some say he hoards treasure only to lure adventurers and heroes into his lair.

description.text: For {3 seconds, white} after using {Grappling Hook, #FBFB79}, your {Movement Speed, white} is increased by {%1.f%%, white} and your {(Weapon Defense), #FCBE7F} and {(Spell Defense), #BA7DFF} are increased by {%.1f%%, white}.

data.name: tiger bug spitter
data.lore: Tigerbug Spitters are rarely seen outside their colonies, as they serve mainly as worker drones. The substance they secrete, once solidified, reinforces the hive's walls and tunnels. However, they are far from defenseless, as this substance also doubles as a highly venomous acid, which they will launch at any threat or intruder.

description.text: Hits from {Multi-Target, #FBFB79} poison the enemy, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} every second for {5 seconds, white}. Additional hits refresh this duration.

data.name: ronan's wraith
data.lore: A Wraith is a vengeful spirit born of intense hatred, despair and unfulfilled desires. The passions that once drove Ronan Shopan in life now bind his restless soul to the mortal plane. Forever cursed by guilt and remorse, he is consumed by a black void where his heart once beat.

description.text: {Multi-Target, #FBFB79} hits no longer deal direct damage, and instead remove one random beneficial effect on each target hit. In case a beneficial effect is successfully removed, the target becomes afflicted by a curse dealing {%.1f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} every {2 seconds, white} for {6 seconds, white}. Only one beneficial  effect can be removed from each individual target.

data.name: black bear
data.lore: Black Bears thrive in forests, hills, and even the edges of human settlements. Though they prefer to avoid confrontation, they can be fiercely territorial when provoked, striking with surprising speed and overwhelming strength. Best left undisturbed, these relentless giants are not to be underestimated.

description.text: {Quick Draw, #FBFB79} can now also inflict a debilitating {bleed, #FC7474} effect on all targets hit, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} every second for a total of {3 seconds, white}.

data.name: hossoc the grievous
data.lore: A brilliant mind poured into one single purpose can achieve the extraordinary, but dwarves know how to hold grudges, and Hossoc was not the exception. What's left for a man who lost everything they held dear, other than cold revenge?

description.text: The second shot from {Quick Draw, #FBFB79} now leaves a bomb at the target's position. The bomb explodes after {2 seconds, white}, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} to targets in a small area around itself.

data.name: morningstar forester
data.lore: Don't mistake the Morningstar Foresters for simple laborers. Every Morningstar expedition consists only of the best and most faithful soldiers, and these men are as skilled at felling trees as they are cutting down any heathen who dares stand in their way.

description.text: {Pirate's Ambrosia, #FBFB79} is now an instant cast and does not heal, instead your {Movement Speed, white} is increased by {%.1f%%, white} for {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: giant jellyfish
data.lore: The Giant Jellyfish has spawned countless tales among sailors, drifting like a phantom beneath the waves. Its translucent form hides tendrils that crackle with shocking fury. Many are the stories of ghostly flashes in the dark water, an omen to turn back the ship before a deadly storm strikes.

description.text: While channeling {Pirate's Ambrosia, #FBFB79}, your {(Weapon Defense), #FCBE7F} and {(Spell Defense), #BA7DFF} are increased by {%.1f%%, white}. This effect can't be dispelled, but will be removed if the channeling is canceled by any means.

data.name: skeleton deathweaver
data.lore: For necromancers, true mastery lies in transcending death itself. But not all who seek lichdom succeed, some falter at the threshold, their souls snared between life and oblivion. Deathweavers are the wretched result of such failures, their mind fractured and twisted beyond recognition, their fractured soul consumed as fuel for their dark sorcery.

description.text: Instead of the base effects, now every enemy standing inside {Smoke Screen, #FBFB79} has their {Healing Received, #77D463} reduced by {35%%, white} and their {(Spell Defense), #BA7DFF} reduced by {10%%, white}. {Smoke Screen, #FBFB79} now lasts for {%.1f seconds, white}.

data.name: poisonpetal rotbloom
data.lore: A rare Poisonpetal variant, the Rotbloom thrives where life withers. Its fleshy petals lure the unwary with a sickly glow, while the twisted maw at its center exhales a creeping miasma. Those who linger too long find themselves choking on its poison, their bodies soon feeding the soil for new blooms to spread.

description.text: Instead of the base effects, {Smoke Screen, #FBFB79} now deals {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} every second to all enemies inside of it. {Smoke Screen, #FBFB79} lasts for {3 seconds, white}.

data.name: mossback giant
data.lore: Where nature has breathed life into stone, the Mossback Giants stand tall as ancient guardians. Hunched and colossal, these beings embody the very essence of the stonelings. Gentle giants when left undisturbed, they wander the rocky expanses, their massive presence echoing the primordial forces that shaped them.

description.text: {Pirate's Parry, #FBFB79} now reduces only 10%% of incoming damage for its duration. When the effect ends, unleash a burst of stone spikes, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} to all targets in a medium area around yourself.

data.name: orc axe tossa'
data.lore: While honor and combat discipline are prized among many orc clans, the Orc Tossa' holds a bloodier view of victory. With savage glee, they hurl axe after axe, wearing their foes down in relentless volleys. Once their prey collapses from exhaustion, the Tossa' pins them to the ground, only to summon their trained wolves to finish the kill.

description.text:
{Pirate's Parry, #FFA851} becomes instant cast and doesn't reduce incoming damage anymore. For the following {3 seconds, white}, any targeted attack that hits the caster triggers a counterattack, launching an air blade back at the attacker, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151}.

This effect can trigger only once per second.

data.name: embercap mushroom
data.lore: The Embercap Mushroom emerges wherever fiery volcanic activity is found. Hardened by the elements, their rock-solid exterior grants them incredible durability against the harsh environment. Scholars are still debating if these creatures are a result of volcanic eruptions, or the catalysts that spark the very events they emerge from.

description.text:
While {Illegal Munitions, #FBFB79} is active, creates a magma pool below your current target every {4 seconds, white} when hitting them with basic attacks.

The magma pool lasts for {2 seconds, white} and deals {%1.f%% (Spell Power) spell damage, #BA7DFF} to enemies standing over it.

data.name: great white shark
data.lore: Many scholars are interested in studying the relationship between Great White Sharks and their more developed and intelligent cousins, the shark-folk. Sailors, however, prefer to deal with the latter, claiming that ?With the shark-folk, at least you can trade gold for your life, the other ones only want your flesh.?

description.text: {Illegal Munitions, #FBFB79} no longer empowers your basic attacks to deal area damage. Instead, make your basic attacks apply Corroded Armor to your targets, reducing their {(Weapon Defense), #FCBE7F} and {(Spell Defense), #BA7DFF} by {%.1f%%, white}. Corroded Armor lasts for {4 seconds, white}.

data.name: mossolith
data.lore: Stealthy and cunning, the Mossolith excels in ambushing prey using its mossy, stone-like exterior for camouflage. Dwelling in rocky terrain, it feeds on small creatures and vegetation. Mossoliths often mark their hunting grounds with distinctive scratches on tree trunks and rocks.

description.text: {Makeshift Grenade, #FBFB79} doesn't burn the area anymore when detonating, instead it explodes releasing a shrapnel barrage, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} in a medium area around itself.

data.name: vermurat
data.lore: Vemurat was the Dragon that fell. Crashing into the Elder Coast, he was drawn in by the boundless wealth of the Dwarven Kingdom, transforming their tunnels and hills into his lair, Crowfell. Once settled, the draconic corruption seeping out of his lair began to work its way across the land, seducing and subjugating those that surrendered to it.

description.text: {Makeshift Grenade, #FBFB79} now has two charges with a cooldown of {40 seconds, white} each. Instead of the base effect, {Makeshift Grenade, #FBFB79} now throws a bomb that detonates after {1 second, white}, dealing {%.1f%% (Weapon Power) weapon damage, #FF5151} in a small area around itself.

data.name: morningstar minuteman
data.lore: They form the backbone of Morningstar expeditions, builders, farmers and settlers in newly claimed lands. But that doesn't make them any less dangerous, as they're also trained sharpshooters always ready to spring into battle, eager to drive back any opposition to the expedition's progress.

description.text: Targets hit by {Target Lock, #FBFB79} have their {(Weapon Power), #FF5151} and {(Spell Power), #BA7DFF} reduced by %.1f%% for {3 seconds, white}.

data.name: thunderflow jackal
data.lore: The Thunderflow Jackal was created by recklessly channeling the destructive power of Manastorm Djinns. A being born of raw energy and primal instinct, it has become a weapon of devastating power, a living storm bound to the will of its creators, if they manage to keep it in check.

description.text: Targets hit by a fully charged {Target Lock, #FBFB79} are {snared, #FFA851} for {%.1f seconds, white}.
